pattern: Building the answer bit by bit from the high end, you greedily assume each bit can be 1 and confirm it whenever two numbers' known prefixes can differ there.
hints: The most significant bit you can force to 1 in some pair's XOR matters more than every lower bit combined, so decide bits from high to low.
Keep only the high prefixes of all numbers; a target XOR bit pattern is achievable if two stored prefixes differ by exactly that pattern.
At each bit, tentatively set it on top of the answer so far and check, using a set of masked prefixes, whether some pair could produce that prefix; keep the bit only if it can.

edge_inputs.description: single element pairs with itself for zero
two values where the high bit dominates
powers of two so the answer is the OR of the two largest distinct bits

stress.note: wide bit range exercises the high-to-low greedy decisions
